summary: Editor window's size is glitching after staying in background for some time

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4
4.5 dev2

### System information

Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-7700HQ CPU @ 2.80GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

When the editor is open, but stays in the background for unspecified amount of time, it can sometimes get glitched. The main screen buttons hide below title bar and the window, which should be maximized, is awkwardly shrunk.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7dca9d7a-f1f1-4909-82d1-6ba3ef94f6cd

Interestingly, when I tried to record it with ShareX, it recognized the window as if it was properly maximized.

Might be related to #97107, but as seen in the video, the buttons can be interacted with, both with shortcuts and clicking.
The issue is fixed by simply dragging the window to reset its size.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open Godot editor
2. Switch to another window and just do something else, while Godot is still in the background, forgotten
3. After some time return to the editor

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: FileDialog.file_selected signal not triggering after exporting project using native dialog (only works in Editor)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in Godot 4.4.1

### System information

Manjaro Linux  kernel 6.6.85-2, Intel i7-4500U

### Issue description

After exporting the project, opening a file using the native FileDialog does not work, the file_selected signal is never triggered.

Inside the editor the signal is triggered as expected and I can open said file in the function I set for that signal. 
For some reason it only does not work after exporting.

This is the only code run by the MRP. In the editor, the label displays the file selected with the native File Dialog. But if I export the project and select any file, nothing happens. The file_selected signal is not triggered or at least the function I am connecting it to.:

```
func _on_button_pressed() -> void:
	var dialog := FileDialog.new()
	dialog.use_native_dialog = true
	dialog.file_mode = FileDialog.FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILE
	dialog.file_selected.connect(_on_file_selected)
	add_child(dialog)
	dialog.visible = true

func _on_file_selected(file: String):
	$Label.text = file
```

I haven't tested this on Windows or Mac. Maybe this issue has something to do with the export templates? At least the Linux one?

If anybody has some idea or some workaround, please let me know. 
Thanks in advance :)

### Steps to reproduce

Works:
- Open the MRP
- Run in the Editor
- Click the button and select a file
- Works as expected ( the label shows the file selected)

Does not work:
- Export the MRP
- Run the exported executable
- Click the button and select a file
- Nothing happens (the label remains empty)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[filedialogtest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19902671/filedialogtest.zip)

summary: Use Jolt Physics by default in newly created projects

description.text:
This also adds infrastructure to make specific settings the default, but only in projects created after this point (so that existing projects are not affected). This can be used for progressive upgrades in the future.

This applies to both the project creation dialog and creating projects through the command line using `touch project.godot`.

- This implements the **first** part of https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12289.

## TODO

- [x] Fix "Files have been modified outside Godot" dialog appearing when new project settings are ensured from an empty `project.godot` file.
  - Using `ProjectSettings::set_setting()` then `save()` did the trick (no need to use `save_custom()`).

summary: Manipulating GPUParticles nodes via inspector crashes engine on Windows 11

description.text:
### Tested versions

Bug encountered using v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6] and using v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6607) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12650H (16 threads)

### Issue description

When using a GPUParticle2D node, the editor might freeze and require to be forcefully closed. It doesn't seem to be dependent on the rest of the project. It only happens when changing value of the GPUParticle2D node via the inspector.

NB: The issue seems very similar to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/72469


### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce
1)  Create a new project
2) Create any root node other than a GPUParticle2D (not sure if that step is required but it might be, it seems like the GPUParticle2D not being the root is important), like a simple Node (what is used).
3) Create a child GPUParticle2D node.
4) Starts changing values in the GPUParticle2D node via the inspector. The bug seems to be triggered by changing values. Keep changing values until bug occurs. The value change doesn't seem to be relevant in triggering the bug.

At some point when changing values the editor will freeze and require to be forcefully closed.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Since it can be reproduced from an empty project I don't think there is any sense in providing an MRP.  Tell me if I'm wrong, I'd be happy to help.

summary: The PopupMenu.id_focused signal has unintuitive conditionallity
Default ENetMultiplayerPeer disconnecting on LAN

description.text:
### Tested versions

-Reproducible in Godot 4.3, 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.dev2

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6590) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-10900K CPU @ 3.70GHz (20 threads)

### Issue description

When creating a server and client for a multiplayer game, I noticed that when I have the client and server running on two separate computers on the same network (LAN) that after a random amount of seconds the client will disconnect from the server without an error on either the client or server. The only information I have to go off of is the method I connected to multiplayer.peer_disconnected which only tells me the peers id. I do not expect this disconnect to happen as neither a crash happens, an error by Godot is never displayed to the editor, nor is multiplayer.multiplayer_peer.disconnect_peer(id) ever called. The ENetMultiplayerPeer for both client and server are using only ENetMultiplayerPeer.new() and do not change any of the settings once created.

When testing the same server/client code on the same machine (Local Host) this random disconnection never happens. 

I've tested it with both the server computer on an Ethernet connection and on a Wi-Fi connection. The client computer is always on a Wi-Fi connection.

In an MRP version I am not sending any RPCs either, just the initial connection from client to server.

Computer 1's specs are as described above
Computer 2's specs are as follows:
Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 with Max-Q Design (NVIDIA; 30.0.15.1169) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8565U CPU @ 1.80GHz (8 threads)


### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP on two separate computers on the same network.
2. Start the server on one computer using the Start Server button displayed in the main scene of the project. Note the IP address printed to the console.
3. On the other computer, change the IP address field returned by ENetClient.gd getIPAddressToConnectTo() to the local IP address of the server computer.
5. Start the client connection using the Start Client (LAN) button displayed in the main scene.
6. Observe that the client successfully connects to the server in the console logs on both client and server. This may take a few seconds occasionally.
7. Observe the Godot's console on both client and server for the disconnect message. I never got the connection to last more than 5 minutes and more often than not it disconnected within 1-2 minutes.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[simpleserverclient.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19898347/simpleserverclient.zip)

The proposal already had a linked PR which added color buttons to the gutter.

The following constructors are supported:

summary: `textureSize` being reported as `(1, 1)` with Forward+ renderer

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in `v4.4.stable.mono.official [4c311cbee]`, `v4.5.dev2.mono.official [af2c71397]` ### System information Godot v4.4.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.4.1) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Ultra (Apple8) - Apple M2 Ultra (24 threads) ### Issue description Yesterday, my game looked/worked fine, then I ran an incremental macOS upgrade (i.e. I was already on Sequoia and probably got a new minor version), and now it seems like `textureSize` is being reported incorrectly in my shaders in Godot. The repro is very simple; attach the shader below, and with the Forward+ renderer, you'll see that `textureSize` reports a `(1, 1)` texture. With the Compatibility renderer, it correctly reports `(128, 128)`: I suspect this is _actually_ a driver issue, but I'm not well-versed in this realm and wanted to file this here just in case it's something that the Godot engine should address. Plus, other people may search for something like this. ### Steps to reproduce - Make a scene with a single `Sprite2D` - Set its texture to the Godot icon (which is 128x128 by default) - Add a `ShaderMaterial` to it with this shader: ```glsl shader_type canvas_item; void vertex() { } void fragment() { vec2 tex_size = vec2(textureSize(TEXTURE, 0)); if (tex_size.x == 1.0) { COLOR = vec4(1.0,0.0,1.0,1.0); } } ``` - Toggle between the Forward+ and Compatibility renderers to see two different results ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [ShaderRepro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19896251/ShaderRepro.zip)

summary: Scale from Cursor option in AnimationPlayer does not scale from cursor

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 
Master
4.4.1.stable
3.6.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6614) - AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

`Scale from Cursor` option does the same as `Scale Selection`. It uses the first key as pivot.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/542bf99c-5b7d-47d0-acb4-c3c9a5497154

This is because last_menu_track_opt is set to `EDIT_SCALE_CONFIRM` by the time it's checked:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/1f787b63a54b74a5238653883fe1a531200b675e/editor/animation_track_editor.cpp#L6728

The actual function for scaling is also incorrect, while the pivot is not set correctly because of the above, it is also not used correctly in the function:

current:
```cpp
#define NEW_POS(m_ofs) (((s > 0) ? m_ofs : from_t + (len - (m_ofs - from_t))) - pivot) * Math::abs(s) + from_t
```

suggested:
```cpp
#define NEW_POS(m_ofs) (((s > 0) ? m_ofs : from_t + (len - (m_ofs - from_t))) - pivot) * Math::abs(s) + pivot
```

### Steps to reproduce

Select some keys and use the function.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

NA

summary: Godot LSP and DAP ports (6006 and 6007) conflict with OpenMPI tool `mpirun`

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4.1 (snap, flatpack, compiled from sources)

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-7940X CPU @ 3.10GHz (14 threads)

### Issue description

When running, Godot prevents from using mpirun on the same machine.



### Steps to reproduce

1. Open a terminal (bash)
2. Type "mpirun". Expected output (showing mpirun works fine) is:
```
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
mpirun could not find anything to do.

It is possible that you forgot to specify how many processes to run
via the "-np" argument.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
```
3. Launch Godot.
4. Create/Open a project.
5. Once the project is fully opened, go back to the bash terminal and type again "mpirun".
Now, there is *no output* and one needs to *kill* mpirun with Ctrl-C (two times) to regain control.
6. Close the project in Godot.
7. Go back to the bash terminal and type again "mpirun". Now the output is the one expected (cf. step 2).

**After investigation**: when launching "mpirun" while a Godot project is opened, this appears in the project's Output window:
```[LSP] Connection Taken```
and when mpirun is killed:
```[LSP] Disconnected```
I do not know exactly what is LSP for but I understood I don't need it. So I deactivated it by editing the `Sconstruct` file in source code, adding:
```env.Append(CPPDEFINES=["GDSCRIPT_NO_LSP"])```
after
```env = Environment(tools=[])```

After compilation with scons, seems LSP is correclty deactivated. But then the message appearing in the project's Output when launching mpirun is:
```[DAP] Connection Taken```
and the result is exactly the same (mpirun stuck and need to kill it two times).


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[new-game-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19894839/new-game-project.zip)

summary: Add missing `backward` flag to TRS3D & BlendShape Track's interpolation in the `AnimationMixer` for Nearest interpolation

description.text:
Context: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/105385

The `backward` flag was added by https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/92861 to handle backward nearest interpolation. It was applied to ValueTrack, but not applied TRS3D & BlendShape Tracks so now apply it to them.

This changes the behavior of the reverse playback when Nearest mode is used in TRS3D & Blend Track, so the break compat label is added (but that means it has been fixed to a more correct and expected result).

In the case:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/95cd478f-3f99-4e43-92ba-10b413b1ba64)

Before:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/357c7790-fe10-447c-a62d-2e2845b645f9

After fix:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ee7c73b-e962-4e55-86a8-175422d7eb53

Note that scrubbing in the editor does not affect it, as it cannot determine if it is playing backwards or not (to see the effect as debugging/testing, you must play it back with play()). This would be consistent behavior with ValueTrack.

summary: Make documentation ignore undocumented private signals

description.text:
Currently, the automatically-generated documentation hides properties, methods, and constants that are "private" (ie underscore-prefixed) and "undocumented" (ie have no description, are deprecated, or are experimental).

However, signals that fulfill these conditions are still displayed.

This PR makes undocumented private signals no longer appear in auto-generated docs, matching the behavior of other member types.

### Convenience MRP

Example project showing each permutation of member type, public/private, and documented/undocumented.

Secondary-click the `DocTestClass` node and select "Open Documentation" from the context menu.

[Undocumented.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19872746/Undocumented.zip)

summary: TileMapLayer draw mode performance is bad on big maps

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.5.dev2.mono, 4.4.1.stable.mono

Godot v4.5.dev2.mono - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700K (24 threads)

### Issue description

On very big tilemaps the performance of the Godot editor heavily drops as soon as you go into the "draw mode". With draw mode i mean that option where it draws the orange tile grid on your tilemap and where you can paint tiles on it via the paint tool, line tool and rectangle tool.
As soon as you leave this draw mode, the performance returns to being good again.
I have pretty good hardware and it will happen at like 15000x15000 tiles for me. Someone on the Godot Discord server said some people on the forums already have this problem at like 500x500 tiles.
The performance will be so bad, that it is basically not really usable anymore.

### Steps to reproduce

- Make a new 2D scene and open it in the 2D view of the Godot editor
- Add a TileMapLayer to the scene
- Create a new TileSet (i use default 16x16px tile size)
- Add a tile atlas image to your created TileSet
- Scroll very far out
- Use the rectangle tool to fill up a huge area of your TileMapLayer with a tile of your choice

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.5.dev2.official [af2c713]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev2

### Issue description

I’ve noticed this since older versions, but now I’d like to point it out clearly. I’m not sure what the intended behavior is, but my assumption is that when you're renaming, the input field is designed to follow you - so you can scroll further down the list and still continue renaming. I also believe this approach is easier than placing an input field on every line in the tree.

However, there are position-related issues. For example, the input field can move outside the line being renamed and appear in the wrong position, which confuses the user. Also, if you select an item and move the cursor using the arrow keys, then scroll the list, the input field shifts to an incorrect location as well.

Overall, I think the correct behavior for this element should be to stay within the bounds of the line being renamed, or stick to the bottom of the list if it has been scrolled far down.

https://youtu.be/d45idu_Xjmg

public void TestReplacement()

    _root.QueueFree();

summary: GDScript static variables do not update during hot-reload (godot 4.3+)

description.text:

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sonoma (14.7.3) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (16 threads)

### Issue description

When GDScripts with static variables are hot-reloaded, the `_static_init()` function is executed, but new instances of the class still get the old values for static variables.  

### Steps to reproduce

* Copy the below script into any godot project
* Add a StaticsReload node to a 3D scene
* Note the addresses reported for the allocated CylinderMesh
    * an address will be reported by `_static_init()`, and `_ready()`
* Edit the GDScript, changing any of the printed messages
    * After saving, the console will create a new CylinderMesh instance in `_static_init()`
* Add another StaticsReload node to the scene
    * The mesh value reported in `_ready()` will match the original value, not the latest one from `_static_init()`

Class to demonstrate the problem
```gdscript
@tool
extends Node3D
class_name StaticsReload

static var mesh : Mesh
static func _static_init() -> void:
	mesh = CylinderMesh.new()
	print_debug("_static_init(): reload created mesh ", mesh)

func _ready() -> void:
	print_debug("_ready(): mesh is ", mesh)
```

_static_init(): reload created mesh <CylinderMesh#-9223369589729969170>
_static_init(): reload created mesh <CylinderMesh#-9223369589595757380>

# Add another `StaticsReload` node, mesh reported does not match latest _static_init() value, only original value
Create Node
_ready(): mesh is <CylinderMesh#-9223369739919606881>
# tried removing all instances and re-adding.
Remove Node(s)

_ready(): mesh is <CylinderMesh#-9223369739919606881>

summary: Components for Node

description.text:
- _Production Edit: Closes_ https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12306

This PR adds components to Node. The implementation is simple for now but there are options to tweak if desired.

Use case:
- It has been a topic of discussion to allow Nodes to inherit or implement >1 "classes" of functionality (e.g. traits, interfaces, multiple inheritance). (See links below.) This solves many cases where this type of modularity is needed/useful by adding Components to all Node types similar to Entity-Component engines (Unreal, Flax, etc.).
- Child Nodes can be used, however, there are limitations to how instanced child scenes can be managed in the inspector. Having the components directly attached to the scene's root Node allows for easy tweaking and access at design time in the inspector and at runtime. There is also a performance concern when using deeply nested Nodes with partial implementations, as the official docs also indicate it is more performant to implement more functionality in a single Node's script rather than separate them across several Nodes' script throughout the hierarchy.

Usage:
- An "Add Component" button was added to the inspector:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c02d6dcd-66e5-4419-8ae8-dedbac64f2a0)
- A dialog gives a drop-down of all Component types available (duplicates are not allowed). An 'X' allows user to remove a component just like metadata:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0317e72c-3477-435b-abd2-d569610dad97)
- User can manage the Component's \@export'ed properties as usual:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/635ff16f-19ae-4a5e-8d79-7c601c694b22)
- Scripts can manage components too. Note for now, a Component must belong to only 1 Node:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/696063d3-7bd4-4bbd-81e7-ad64c76db75b)
- GDExtension and GDScripts, of course, can override the necessary Node notification callbacks:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/314f4824-61cd-4731-aeec-bcaab8a79ee8)


Implementation overview:
- An Actor class extends Object. Node extends Actor. Actor handles most of the component-related details. components are stored in a HashMap similar to Object::metadata. Duplicates of the same Component type are not allowed; the keys use the most derived global_class_name of the Component's script or the class_name of the C++ class.
- Node manages the Node/SceneTree-specific notifications for its components (\_process(), \_input(), etc.). Node also sets set_processing_...(bool) accordingly to allow the components' callbacks to execute if overridden. This means that, for example, set_processing_input(true) will be true if >= 1 attached component overrides _input(event), and the Node's script (if one is attached) doesn't necessarily override _input(event).
- Component extends Resource to allow serialization of properties and for using \@export annotation.

Some initial considerations:
- Currently, HashSets hold each Component based on the notifications they handle (e.g., if they override \_process(), etc.). This allows for O(1) add/erase but doesn't guarantee any execution order of the overridden function. This means if 2 Components both handle the same input action, it is unknown which Component would mark it as handled on the viewport.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/4872

https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/6416

[Edit] Changed title as I realized these are not script components as they don't need scripts (as defined in Godot) to implement their functionality - they can be built-in or native types instead.

summary: [LineEdit/TextEdit] Add composite character backspace delete and get composite character positions

description.text:
Adds property to disable behavior of `LineEdit`, `TextEdit` and `CodeEdit` where backspace deletes into the mid grapheme of a composite character like ❤️‍🩹. Preserves backward compatibility by adding property `backspace_deletes_composite_character_enabled` disabled by default.

Also adds methods to correctly locate the column or caret location at the start or end of a composite character. This is required in order to detect in script, for example, that a composite character was typed by a user.

Backspace in `LineEdit` and `TextEdit` before PR fix. The backspace key must be hit three times to delete all the way across the composite character:

summary: Adding collision shapes to bodies already in scene tree is slow when using Jolt Physics

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Windows 10 pro 22H2 - Godot 4.4.1.stable - Forward+ - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT

### Issue description

I am developing a blocky/voxel based game that can load thousands of box shaped collisions in a single scene. When I now tried switching to Jolt physics for the first time the first thing I noticed is that my scene went from being loaded in aprox. 2 seconds to take a bit over a minute.

In the MRP I attach the issue is more obvious because it doesn't load anything other than collisions. It creates a scene with 15000 box shaped collisions. With the default physics engine it loads basically instantly. With the Jolt physics engine it takes more than 30 seconds in my system.

### Steps to reproduce

Download the attached MRP, unzip it, open the project.

PLEASE NOTICE that if "Visible Collision Shapes" is not enabled, then the project will not render anything except an FPS counter. But I recommend to leave "Visible Collision Shapes" disabled when testing this just in case in interferes with loading times.

1. The project is using the default physics engine. So when you hit the play button you should be able to almost instantly see the FPS counter running, meaning that the scene is loaded with the 15000 collisions.
2. Switch to Jolt physics Engine (Project Settings -> Physics -> 3D -> Physics Engine = Jolt Physics). Save the project and restart Godot.
3. Now hit the play button. I would expect it to take a few seconds, noticeable more than in the previous scenario, depending on your system.
4. Now you can enable  "Visible Collision Shapes" and play again just so you can see what collisions are actually being loaded.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[collisions.performance.MRP-4_4_1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19848680/collisions.performance.MRP-4_4_1.zip)

summary: Incorrect InputEventKey for `shift+key`

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.official.49a5bc7b6

Should probably happen in all versions by checking git history.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900H (20 threads)

### Issue description

When using the Web export, `InputEventKey` reports an incorrect keycode for shifted number keys (Shift + 1 through Shift + 9)

**web `shift-1`**
```
EventInputEventKey: keycode=33 (Exclam), mods=Shift, physical=false
```

**desktop `shift-1`**
```
EventInputEventKey: keycode=49 (1), mods=Shift, physical=false
````

This makes key remapping and game input inconsistent between platforms.  When mapping keys for input in a game, pressing `Shift + 1` should be stored and displayed as `Shift + 1`, not `Shift + !`, since the player is pressing the `1` key (regardless of layout or symbol output). This works correctly on Desktop build.


### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
func _unhandled_input(event):
	if event is InputEventKey and event.pressed:
		print("Event", event)
		print("Keycode: ", event.keycode)
		print("Physical: ", event.physical_keycode)
		print("Key Label: ", event.key_label)
		print("Unicode: ", event.unicode)
		print("Location: ", event.location)
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Default project with above `func _unhandled_input`

summary: Add drag zoom feature with CTRL+MiddleMouseButton

description.text:
Zoom drag feature was only available in 3D editor (viewport). In this commit I added the same functionality to other editors as well, so you can zoom drag in any of them: 2D editor, animation track editor, visual shader editor, etc.

This keeps Godot consistent and immensely helps with 2D game development. My mouse wheel doesn't work well, I needed this change so much. And since I use it all the time in other software like Photoshop, I made this PR. You can pan using middle click and zoom by adding CTRL to the combo.

Bellow is a demo for the 2D editor:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3cc8076-2de9-4c54-a48d-44bfa2328d2a

summary: Allow Inspector Section Checkboxes to hide features, Add "On" text to checkboxes

description.text: Followup to #105272 This PR creates a new optional hint string for the `PROPERTY_HINT_GROUP_ENABLE` hint: `"feature"`. This hint string suggests to the section that if the checkbox is not enabled, then it can hide its contents. This reduces complexity in cases where the variables that are only meaningful when the feature is active. In the past, such instances needed to use `_validate_property` in order to achieve this functionality, but with this PR that code can be replaced with the "feature" hint string. With this PR, adding toggle-able feature sections is reduced to: ```gdscript @export_group("Collision", "collision_") @export_custom(PROPERTY_HINT_GROUP_ENABLE, "feature") var collision_enabled = false ``` This PR also adds a localized `On` text to the checkboxes in order to increase clickable area, similar to the current checkboxes. This PR also enables checkboxes to work in the `Favorites` tab, and adds section checkboxes to more classes where it is appropriate.

summary: BoneAttachment3D snaps to bone position only after reloading the scene window

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4stable and 4.3stable
- Not reproducible in 4.2.2stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

When you configure **Bone Name** for a BoneAttachment3D node, the node doesn't update its position to the configured bone. It only snaps to the bone after you open another scene or otherwise reload the window of the scene which has the BoneAttachment3D node. 

Moreover, if you configure **Bone Name** and then change the position of the node before changing the window, that transform is applied to the bone position, rather than the original position of the node. This means if you try to manually position the node to the bone, relaoding the window causes the node to be far away from your intial transform.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Import a .glb file with bones into a scene
2. Make children editable on the node, then add a BoneAttachment3D node to the Skeleton3D node.
3. Select any bone for the BoneAttachment3D **Bone Node** setting. At this point, you should see that the node has not snapped to the bone as expected. 
4. Open a new tab in the editor, then return to the scene. You should see that the node has now snapped to the bone as expected. 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[minimumprojectbonebug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19833700/minimumprojectbonebug.zip)
